zh.lifeSummary: 當James Allen於1671年2月2日出生在美國緬因州約克吉特利時，他的父親Walter Allen26歲，母親Elizabeth Middleton22歲。他於大約1701年與Dorothy Barsham在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1734年之後在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克貝維其可，埋葬在美國緬因州約克吉特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Allen于1671年2月2日出生在美国缅因州約克吉特利时，他的父亲Walter Allen26岁，母亲Elizabeth Middleton22岁。他于大约1701年与Dorothy Barsham在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1734年之后在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克貝維其可，埋葬在美国缅因州約克吉特利。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1713: Massachusetts, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1716: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

en.lifeSummary: When James Allen was born on 2 February 1671, in Kittery, York, Maine, United States, his father, Walter Allen, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth Middleton, was 22. He married Dorothy Barsham about 1701, in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died after 1734, in Berwick, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Kittery, York, Maine, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Allen est né le 2 février 1671, à Kittery, York, Maine, États-Unis, son père, Walter Allen, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Middleton, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Dorothy Barsham environ 1701, à Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé après 1734, à Berwick, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, et a été enterré à Kittery, York, Maine, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Allen am 2. Februar 1671 in Kittery, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter Allen 26 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Middleton 22. Er hat um 1701 in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America Dorothy Barsham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb nach 1734 in Berwick, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Kittery, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Allen è nato il il 2 febbraio 1671 nel Kittery, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Walter Allen, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Middleton, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Dorothy Barsham circa 1701, nel Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì dopo il 1734, nel Berwick, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, e fu sepolto a Kittery, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: James Allenが1671年2月2日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークキッテリーで生まれた時，父Walter Allenは26歳，母Elizabeth Middletonは22歳であった。約1701年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムポルトスモウスでDorothy Barshamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1734年以後にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヨークバーウィックで死去し，アメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークキッテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스

ko.Kittery, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크 키터이
ko.Berwick, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크 버윅
ko.lifeSummary: James Allen(이)가 1671년 2월 2일 미국 메인 주 요르크 키터이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter Allen(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Middleton(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1701년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스에서 Dorothy Barsham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1734년 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크 버윅에서 사망했으며 미국 메인 주 요르크 키터이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Allen nasceu em 2 de fevereiro de 1671, em Kittery, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Walter Allen, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Middleton, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Dorothy Barsham aproximadamente 1701, em Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu após 1734, em Berwick, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, e foi sepultado em Kittery, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Allen родился 2 февраля 1671 г. в местности киттири, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Walter Allen было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Middleton, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothy Barsham около 1701 г. в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер после 1734 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика и был похоронен в местности киттири, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Allen nació el 2 de febrero de 1671, en Kittery, York, Maine, Estados Unidos, su papá, Walter Allen, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Middleton, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Dorothy Barsham aproximadamente 1701, en Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió después de 1734, en Berwick, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, y fue sepultado en Kittery, York, Maine, Estados Unidos.
